meaning: Size of the original study (more participants = larger value)
scale: log10 of the original-study sample size n

meaning: Strength of the originally reported evidence (larger = smaller p-value)
scale: two-sided |z| reconstructed from the original p-value; |z|=Phi^-1(1 - p/2); capped near 8
notes: z=1.96 corresponds to p=.05; z=3.29 to p=.001

meaning: How surprising / counterintuitive the focal finding is judged to be (higher = more surprising)
scale: integer LLM rating on a ~1-7 scale (observed 2-7)

meaning: How adequately powered/sized the study is judged to be (higher = more adequate)
scale: (LLM sample-size ordinal 1-5 + any-power-analysis flag 0/1) / 6; takes values 0.167 .. 1.0
notes: Combines perceived sample size and presence of a power analysis

meaning: Whether the paper reports an internal (within-paper) replication of the focal effect
Whether the design is interventional/experimental with a post-treatment outcome measure
Whether the focal effect size is from a directional family
scale: 1 = r / Cohen's d / Hedges' g / odds ratio / z; 0 = other (e.g. variance-explained, nonparametric)

meaning: Analytic multiplicity: log of (1 + number of F-tests reported)
scale: natural log of (1 + count of 'F(' occurrences); log1p compresses the heavy right tail
spread: more than half are 0 (exact SD computed in script)
notes: Replaces raw n_f_tests_tei because of severe right-skew; higher = more reported F-tests
